name: Junbo He
title: AIPM / AI-native product explorer
tagline: I design AI-native product loops that turn demand signals into clear product decisions and working interfaces.
worldview: I am building a quiet system for studying what deserves to exist, narrowing scope, and shipping the clearest next artifact.
bio: I am a computer science student building in public while exploring what product work looks like in the AI era.
Right now my center of gravity is a gym-focused fitness app used as a proving ground for subtraction, signal capture, and AI-assisted delivery.

steps.title: Capture live demand
steps.description: I collect recurring user needs, complaints, and high-frequency jobs before deciding what deserves product attention.

steps.title: Compress into judgement
steps.description: I pressure-test user value, scope boundaries, and product logic until the direction becomes simpler and harder to misread.

steps.title: Translate into interface
steps.description: I move structured intent into markdown, interface systems, and code so the distance between idea and surface stays short.

thinkingQuestions.prompt: What kind of product judgement becomes more valuable, not less, in an AI-native workflow?
How far should a gym-focused app go before it stops being meaningfully focused?
What does a strong AIPM personal brand look like when it is built on real shipping instead of abstract claims?
